reference_data.note: Golden peaks derived from validated spectra and literature-supported assignments

critical_peaks.label: benzene ring breathing
critical_peaks.justification: Most dominant and reproducible Raman peak for methamphetamine

critical_peaks.label: C-C and C-N stretching
critical_peaks.justification: Key differentiating peak from amphetamine

intensity_model.rule: Intensity contributes proportionally; low intensity peaks are not eliminated
intensity_model.note: Intensity depends on concentration and instrument sensitivity

shift_model.rule: Closer peaks have higher contribution; farther peaks reduce confidence

overlap_handling.rule: If peak matches multiple drugs, assign score based on proximity
overlap_handling.example: 1025 peak is ambiguous between meth (1019) and amphetamine (1032)

confusion_analysis.similarity: Both share strong ~1000 cm⁻¹ aromatic peak
confusion_analysis.difference: Meth shows 1019 peak; amphetamine shows 1032 peak
Cocaine has strong carbonyl peak (~1700 cm⁻¹)

rules: At least two critical or high-confidence peaks should match
Presence of 986 + 1001 + 1019 strongly confirms meth signature
Missing one critical peak reduces confidence but does not reject
Single peak match is insufficient
Pattern match increases confidence significantly
Conflicting peaks reduce score
if golden signaure matches most then only one critical peak presence is sufficient for identification

notes: Methamphetamine shows a strong aromatic Raman signature dominated by ~1001 cm⁻¹ peak, supported by 1019 and 986 peaks, and absence of carbonyl (~1700 cm⁻¹)

rules: At least two critical or high-confidence peaks should match

critical_peaks.label: phenyl ring breathing

intensity_model.rule: Intensity contributes proportionally; weak peaks are not discarded

shift_model.note: SERS may introduce small peak shifts

overlap_handling.penalty: reduce confidence if no supporting peaks present

confusion_analysis.difference: Meth has 1019; fentanyl has 1033 and strong 1457 peak
resolution_strategy: Presence of 1457 and broad 1200–1600 peaks → fentanyl

confusion_analysis.difference: Cocaine has strong carbonyl (~1700 cm⁻¹)

rules: Presence of 1001 + 1033 strongly supports fentanyl
At least two critical/high peaks should match
Broad peak distribution (1200–1600 region) increases confidence
Missing one critical peak reduces confidence but does not reject
Single peak match is insufficient
Conflicting peaks reduce score
if golden signature matches most then only one critical peak presence is sufficient for identification

notes: Fentanyl is characterized by a strong doublet near 1000–1030 cm⁻¹ and broad supporting peaks in 1200–1600 cm⁻¹ region, distinguishing it from methamphetamine and cocaine

overlap_handling.penalty: do not use 1000 peak alone for identification

Cocaine has strong 1000 + 1700 combination

At least two critical/high peaks required
1000 peak alone is insufficient
Pattern match (tropane + ester) increases confidence
Missing 1700 reduces confidence significantly

if golden signature matches most then only one critical peak presence is sufficient for identification
